title: Coastal family and waypoint phase
summary: After the new bluff, beach, tundra, and forest waypoint passes land, lane 3 should deepen them into clearer mini-families. This wave connects the front-half coast spaces into a more coherent sheltered rise family, then adds one calmer rest or recovery follow-on to the newer waypoint spaces so they feel like habitats instead of isolated traversal tricks.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a coastal family follow-on handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The front-half spaces will be more interesting, but they still need to read like one little family instead of isolated proofs.
A connected shelter or rise family would make the early coast more memorable to explore.
The coast should stay broad, cozy, and readable rather than getting fiddly.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a waypoint rest or recovery follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: After the coastal family lands, the newer observation nooks should gain one more calming habitat beat.

execution_order.goal: Implement the waypoint rest or recovery follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: A small rest or recovery seam makes the newer spaces feel like habitats instead of traversal curiosities.

execution_order.goal: Review the waypoint rest or recovery follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 should keep adding wonder, not accumulating route noise.

guardrails: Reuse the current platform, climbable, and terrain systems only.
Keep every new beat recoverable and cozy.
Favor habitat identity and rest over raw height or depth growth.
Do not add another cue language or persistent traversal HUD.
For main-203, keep the pass in coastal-scrub's left windbreak handoff instead of widening beach or corridor runtime.
For main-204, keep the pass in coastal-scrub's right swale-to-pine release instead of revisiting the repaired left map-post band or widening into forest-edge travel.
